title: Inspect the recent GLM bootstrap failure mode from local scripts and likely logs, and return the shortest recovery path or why degraded mode is currently justified. No edits.
summary_text: **Findings** - The recent degraded event is recorded only in repo-local state for run `adhoc:MacBook-Pro-M5-5:kernel-workspace:53578:run:20260406T163904:58020`: GLM failure (`glm-command-exit-1`) at `2026-04-06T07:40:30Z`, then state moved to `degraded-allowed` with reason `bootstrap-second-glm-failure` at `2026-04-06T07:44:25Z` ([runtime-ledger.json](/Users/masayuki/Dev/fugue-orchestrator-public/.fugue/kernel-state/runtime-ledger.json#L1356), [glm-run-state.json](/Users/masayuki/Dev/fugue-orchestrator-public/.fugue/kernel-state/glm-run-state.json#L249), [compact](/Users/masayuki/Dev/fugue-orchestrator-public/.fugue/kernel-state/compact/adhoc_MacBook-Pro-M5-5_kernel-workspace_53578_run_20260406T163904_58020.json)). - Canonical state appears split: default state root logic prefers `~/.config/kernel` ([kernel-state-paths.sh](/Users/masayuki/Dev/fugue-orchestrator-public/scripts/lib/kernel-state-paths.sh#L49)), but scheduler defaults to repo `.fugue/kernel-state` ([kernel-scheduler.sh](/Users/masayuki/Dev/fugue-orchestrator-public/scripts/kernel-scheduler.sh#L11)). In `~/.config/kernel`, the same run is still `running` and has no GLM failure recorded ([runtime-ledger.json](/Users/masayuki/.config/kernel/runtime-ledger.json), [compact](/Users/masayuki/.config/kernel/compact/adhoc_MacBook-Pro-M5-5_kernel-workspace_53578_run_20260406T163904_58020.json)). - Likely failure mode is wrapper-level exit `1` (not a modeled semantic failure): `kernel-glm-exec` marks any nonzero wrapper exit as `glm-command-exit-*` ([kernel-glm-exec.sh](/Users/masayuki/Dev/fugue-orchestrator-public/scripts/lib/kernel-glm-exec.sh#L154)), and local `glm` wrapper uses heredoc/help path ([glm](/Users/masayuki/bin/glm#L10)); in this environment it reproduces `cannot create temp file for here document` with exit `1`. **Shortest recovery path** 1. Unify state roots first (this is the shortest/highest-leverage fix): set both `KERNEL_STATE_ROOT` and `KERNEL_SUBSTRATE_STATE_ROOT` to the same path (prefer `~/.config/kernel`). 2. Re-run one GLM smoke through the same bootstrap path (`kernel-glm-exec`/bootstrap runner) and confirm a `glm` success record. 3. If you must continue the already-degraded `.fugue` run, do `glm-reset` for that run; `recover` alone does not clear degraded mode ([kernel-glm-run-state.sh](/Users/masayuki/Dev/fugue-orchestrator-public/scripts/lib/kernel-glm-run-state.sh#L117), [kernel-glm-run-state.sh](/Users/masayuki/Dev/fugue-orchestrator-public/scripts/lib/kernel-glm-run-state.sh#L146)). If you keep using `.fugue/kernel-state` and `glm` keeps exiting `1`, degraded mode is justified. If you use `~/.config/kernel` as canonical, current degraded mode is not justified by the active ledger.
